county_metadata.record_losses: Many court records missing for unknown reasons. No courthouse fire documented.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Drawn from northern third of Lincoln County December 12, 1842. Newton selected as county seat January 8, 1845. German, Swiss, and Scotch-Irish settlers from 1700s. Catawba Indian Nation territory.
county_metadata.research_tips: Start with Lincoln County records pre-1842. Check microfilm at Catawba County Public Library (Rhodes Room). Missing court records may require checking Lincoln County originals. Heritage book in 2 volumes (1986/2006) by Catawba County Genealogical Society.
county_metadata.heritage_book: The Heritage of Catawba County, North Carolina (Vols 1-2, 1986/2006, Catawba County Genealogical Society)

source_profiles.coverage_notes: General Index to Deeds 1837-1926 also available. Complete from formation.

source_profiles.source_name: Catawba County Wills and Estate Papers 1663-1978
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will Index 1843-1966, Wills 1843-1939, Inventories & Sales 1843-1868.
Marriage Books 1-14 with index. Complete from formation.
1,737 issues digitized.

county_metadata.record_losses: 2010 courthouse fire damaged third floor and clock tower; records stored separately and survived. Many court records missing for unknown reasons.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Named for William Pitt, 1st Earl of Chatham. Pittsboro designated county seat 1787. Colonial-era Orange County records essential for pre-1771 ancestors. Historic 1881 courthouse damaged in 2010 fire.
county_metadata.research_tips: Check Orange County records pre-1771. Court of Pleas & Quarter Sessions records 1774-1800 at NCGenWeb. Chatham County Historical Association provides assistance. ROD online from 1771. Heritage materials at Chatham Community Library.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: images and indexes; all recorded documents 1771-present

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Complete online access to deeds, mortgages, plats from formation.

source_profiles.source_name: Chatham County Wills and Estate Records 1779-1868
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, inventories, guardian records. Abstracts 1798-1857 published.

source_profiles.source_name: Chatham County Court of Pleas and Quarter Sessions 1774-1800
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Court orders, legal documents from colonial period. Records 1790-1822.
2,759+ issues digitized.

county_metadata.record_losses: THREE courthouse fires: May 4, 1865 (Union Col. Kirk burned Murphy courthouse), December 12, 1895 (accidental fire), January 16, 1926 (janitor's match). Significant record gaps. Fourth courthouse (1927) built with inflammable blue marble.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Westernmost NC county. Named for Cherokee Indians; on Cherokee lands. Murphy is westernmost county seat. First courts held at Fort Butler and private homes. Probate records begin 1848 (gap from 1839 formation). Marriage records from 1868 only.
county_metadata.research_tips: Start with Macon County pre-1839 records. Land records began 1838, pre-dating county formation. Military/federal records may substitute for lost county records. Federal census critical source. Heritage book by Nell White (1987, 609 pages). Cherokee County Historical Museum.
county_metadata.heritage_book: The Heritage of Cherokee County, North Carolina (Nell A. White, Cherokee County Historical Museum, 1987)

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deeds from 1838 (pre-formation, transferred from Macon). Index 1839-1865.
source_profiles.survival_notes: 1839 onward; earlier deeds in Macon County.

source_profiles.source_name: Cherokee County Wills and Estate Papers 1663-1978
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records from 1848 (9-year gap from formation). Earlier wills in Macon County.
Marriage records from 1868 (29 years after formation). Pre-1868 in Macon County.
Cherokee Herald (1874-1876, 5 issues), Cherokee Scout (1888-1971, 2,510 issues).

county_metadata.record_losses: Records deliberately destroyed by acting clerk of court in 1848. First surviving will book begins 1760; court dockets 1757; county court minutes 1762. NOT a courthouse fire — records were intentionally destroyed.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: One of NC's oldest counties — began as Shaftesbury Precinct 1668 in Albemarle County, renamed Chowan Precinct ~1681, county status 1739. Edenton designated county seat 1722. Historic Chowan County Courthouse (1767) is oldest NC courthouse still standing. Cross-reference with Currituck, Pasquotank, Perquimans (sister precincts from 1689 division).
county_metadata.research_tips: HIGH VALUE colonial county. Deed records from 1695 are exceptional. Margaret M. Hofmann's 'Chowan Precinct, NC, 1696-1723' provides deed abstracts. St. Paul's Episcopal Church (oldest NC church) records valuable. Cross Index to Deeds 1695-1878 is essential starting point. Multiple published abstracts available through Heritage Books. Check pre-1681 Albemarle County records.
county_metadata.heritage_book: Multiple titles including published deed abstracts, marriage records 1742-1868, and early wills 1690-1760 (Heritage Books, Inc.)
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: images and indexes; deeds 1699-1960+, cross index 1695-1878

source_profiles.source_name: Chowan County Deeds 1695-1960 with Cross Index
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Cross Index to Deeds 1695-1878. Deeds 1699-1960. Indices by grantor/grantee 1695-1928. Extraordinary early records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Exceptional survival despite 1848 deliberate destruction of other records.

source_profiles.source_name: Chowan County Wills and Estate Papers 1663-1978
source_profiles.coverage_notes: First surviving will book 1760. Abstracts begin 1707. Probate records 1694-1830. Published early wills 1690-1760.
Published marriage records 1742-1868. Official records 1768+. Complete from 1742.
Deed Book B-1 contains earliest court records Jan 1714/15-Jul 1719. Dockets from 1757, county minutes 1762, Superior Court 1768.
4,488+ issues online. Comprehensive collection.

county_metadata.record_losses: Courthouse fire May 20, 1870 — escaped jail inmate set fire, destroyed all early county records. Deeds from 1870 only, marriage records from 1879, vital records from 1913.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: NC's smallest mountain county. Formed during Civil War from Cherokee County. Pre-1870 records must be searched in Cherokee and Macon counties.
county_metadata.research_tips: For pre-1870, search Cherokee County records. FamilySearch has Clay County Marriage Books 1870-1970. County heritage book has 320+ family genealogies. 'A History of Clay County' by Guy Padgett (1976) also available.
county_metadata.heritage_book: Clay County Heritage (Vol I, Clay County Heritage Book Committee)
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: images and indexes; land records, deeds, mortgages, plats

source_profiles.source_name: Clay County Register of Deeds Land Records 1870-present
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Land transactions, mortgages, deeds of trust, plats. Complete from 1870.

source_profiles.repository: FamilySearch / Clay County Clerk of Court
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estate files, probate court records from 1862.
Marriage records from 1870 forward at county level.
Partial digitization. Clay County Courier 1904-1907 also available.

county_metadata.record_losses: No known major losses. Records relatively complete from formation.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed January 11, 1841 from equal parts Rutherford and Lincoln. Named for Col. Benjamin Cleveland, Revolutionary War hero at Battle of King's Mountain. Shelby became county seat.
county_metadata.research_tips: Excellent record continuity 1841-present. ROD online system has records back to 1945 digitized; office records from 1792 (predecessor county records). Use FamilySearch for tax lists 1823-1896. Heritage book Vol I (1982, 696 pages) and Vol II (2004). Shelby Daily Star on DigitalNC from 1923.
county_metadata.heritage_book: The Heritage of Cleveland County (Vol I, 1982; Vol II, 2004, Cleveland County Historical Association)
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: images and indexes; online from 1945; office records from 1792

source_profiles.source_name: Cleveland County Register of Deeds Online
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Online from 1945. Office records from 1792 (predecessor county). Deeds, mortgages, liens.

source_profiles.source_name: Cleveland County Wills and Probate Records 1795-present
source_profiles.repository: FamilySearch / Cleveland County Clerk of Court
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estate papers, probate records, guardianships. May include Rutherford County probate records pre-1841.
Tax lists, property assessments. Complete series.
Good coverage 1923-1984 on DigitalNC. Cleveland Star (earlier title) also available.

county_metadata.record_losses: No known major losses. Continuous records from formation.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Southeastern Coastal Plain county. Formed December 15, 1808 from Bladen and Brunswick after residents requested easier courthouse access. Whiteville established as county seat 1832 (named after Senator James B. White).
county_metadata.research_tips: Good record continuity 1808-present. Land records begin 1802 (pre-formation, likely Bladen). Probate from 1808, court records from 1817. The News Reporter (Whiteville, founded 1896) has near-complete digitized run 1924-1984 on DigitalNC. For pre-1808, check Bladen and Brunswick counties.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: images and indexes; deeds, mortgages, liens, plats, vital records

source_profiles.source_name: Columbus County Register of Deeds Online
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deeds, mortgages, liens, plats. Pre-1808 records relate to Bladen/Brunswick area lands.

source_profiles.source_name: Columbus County Probate and Estate Records 1808-present
source_profiles.repository: FamilySearch / Columbus County Clerk of Court
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Record of Wills 1808-1908, Records of Executors/Administrators/Guardians 1857-1956.
Marriage licenses from 1867. Birth/death from 1913.
Near-complete run 1924-1984 digitized. Partial 1905-1923.
